learning_outcomes.description: Analyse financial data in order to assess a particular investment or investment strategy (including conducting a valuation of an investment).
Complete authentic investment analysis and have the confidence, creativity and resilience to deal with common industry challenges.
Work effectively in a team environment completing an authentic investment analysis and making a recommendation.
Have the confidence to engage effectively with industry and to persuasively communicate complex ideas verbally and in writing.
Evaluate empirical evidence on market efficiency (globally, over decades) and foresee the implications for investment and portfolio management.
Understand, assess and have formed a view of the social and ethical trends that are impacting investment markets.
Describe techniques for portfolio creation, select and calculate the appropriate techniques to measure portfolio performance.

assessments.task_description:
Weeks 2-6 you will be required to complete tutorial tasks that will assist you in preparing for the team assignment.

The tutorial tasks are due on the Monday after the tutorials are held.

Therefore, you have the week, the weekend and Monday mornings to work on the tutorial tasks.

assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Investment Valuation Project Part A - Written Report and Financial Model
assessments.due_date: Written Report, Excel Model, Presentation Slides 23/10/2026 5:00 pm
assessments.task_description:
The investment valuation project has three parts. Part A - A group written report and financial model, Part B - Presentation, and Part C - Individual Reflection

Part A

  • Financial Model (team score - out of 15)
  • Written report (team score - out of 25)

Peer Evaluation (Parts A and B)

Your peer evaluation will be completed using BuddyCheck. The score you receive is based on feedback from your teammates and will be used to adjust your individual mark for the team assignment. Specifically, your peer evaluation score will be applied to your team score to calculate your final individual result.

Important:

You must complete the peer evaluation to be eligible to receive a mark for the team assignment. Further details on the peer evaluation process and assessment criteria will be provided in the project materials on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Investment Valuation Project Part B - Presentation
assessments.due_date:
Week 13 - Week 13

Team presentations will be held between 8:00 AM and 6:00 PM in Week 13. A detailed schedule will be provided on Blackboard.
assessments.task_description:
Part B

The presentation consists of two components with Q&A.

The In-Person Presentation: Presented during Week 13 between 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM. More details of scheduling will be provided on the course Blackboard site. Presentations are timed, and over-time presentations will be cut short with a 10% presentation score penalty.

  • Professional Presentation (team score - out of 10): Professional presentation across the model, report and the presentation. Industry standard is expected.
  • Individual Presentation (individual score - out of 10): Each team member will receive a unique score out of 10 for their in-class presentation.

Peer Evaluation (Parts A and B)

Your peer evaluation will be completed using BuddyCheck. The score you receive is based on feedback from your teammates and will be used to adjust your individual mark for the team assignment. Specifically, your peer evaluation score will be applied to your team score to calculate your final individual result.

Important:

You must complete the peer evaluation to be eligible to receive a mark for the team assignment. Further details on the peer evaluation process and assessment criteria will be provided in the project materials on Blackboard.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: Presentations are timed, and over-time presentations will be cut short with a 10% presentation score penalty.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Investment Valuation Project Part C - Individual Reflection
assessments.task_description:
Part C

Final Journal Entry (individual score - out of 5): Students must complete a reflection on what they have learnt working on this team based project.
